title: Smoke-test Python BLE LCI transport on real devices
description: Validate project-LICHEN-py4c.1.5 against physical BLE stacks and firmware. Scope: with a LICHEN native BLE LCI device, verify Linux BlueZ and macOS CoreBluetooth scanning, native LCI UUID discovery, optional explicit legacy NUS mode on boards configured for CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_BLE_LEGACY_NUS, MTU chunked writes, TX notifications, reconnect after disconnect, and user-selection behavior. Record devices, OS versions, firmware commit, observed MTU, and any pairing/security prompts. This is blocked on physical BLE hardware/firmware and real host BLE adapters.
notes: Blocked on unavailable physical BLE validation hardware: needs a LICHEN native BLE LCI device/firmware, Linux BlueZ host adapter, and macOS CoreBluetooth host to verify real scanning, pairing prompts, native LCI UUID discovery, explicit legacy NUS mode, ATT MTU/max-write behavior, TX notifications, disconnect/reconnect, and user-selection flows. No physical radios/phones/boards are available in this session; software fake-client coverage is handled in parent project-LICHEN-py4c.1.5.
